relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Herod reaches against the assembly with harm.
James, brother of John, is killed by sword.
Political approval leads to Peter’s arrest during Unleavened Bread.
Peter is guarded intensely until after Passover.
Prison holds Peter, while earnest prayer holds him before the Divine.
The night before exposure, Peter sleeps chained between soldiers.
A messenger and light enter the cell; chains fall.
Peter is dressed for departure and told to follow.
He follows as if through vision, not yet knowing the reality.
Guards and iron gate give way, and the messenger departs.
Peter comes to himself and knows rescue from Herod’s hand.
He goes to Mary’s house where many are praying.
Rhoda answers the gate.
Joy recognizes Peter’s voice before the door is opened.
The praying community struggles to believe the answered prayer.
Peter keeps knocking until amazement opens the door.
He tells the rescue and sends word to James and the siblings.
Daylight brings disturbance among the soldiers.
Herod searches, judges the guards, and goes to Caesarea.
Political dependence seeks peace through court access.
Herod appears in royal display and public speech.
The crowd gives him divine voice language.
Refusing to give glory to the Divine, Herod collapses under judgment.
But the word of the Divine grows and multiplies.
Barnabas and Saul complete the service and take John Mark.
